body_txt: A trope is a storytelling device or convention, a shortcut for describing situations the storyteller can reasonably assume the audience will recognize. Tropes are the means by which a story is told by anyone who has a story to tell. We collect them, for the fun involved. Tropes are not the same thing as cliches. They may be brand new but seem trite and hackneyed; they may be thousands of years old but seem fresh and new. They are not bad, they are not good; tropes are tools that the creator of a work of art uses to express their ideas to the audience. It's pretty much impossible to create a story without tropes . Here is a list of indexes, split into conceptual groups. At the bottom of each trope page you will find a convenient Next or Previous link that allows browsing alphabetically. This is a long list of all tropes we have already catalogued . You may, however, want to take along a supply of Narnia Time in either case.
